title: Launch Series Bible workspace with shared canon across multiple stories
description:
## Problem
Writers creating trilogies or connected universes cannot manage shared canon across stories in one place. This leads to continuity mistakes, duplicate worldbuilding data, and high manual overhead when reusing characters, locations, and rules.

## Target user
Series fiction writers (2+ manuscripts in one universe) who need a single source of truth for canon while drafting individual books.

## Outcome / KPI
- Increase percentage of active users who create a second manuscript in the app
- Reduce continuity-related revisions reported during later draft stages
- Increase reuse rate of existing worldbuilding entities across stories

## Scope
- Add a `Series` container that can include multiple stories
- Create a Series Bible view with shared entities (characters, locations, lore/rules)
- Allow linking shared entities into scenes from any story in the series
- Show cross-story references on each shared entity (where it appears)
- Add guardrail warning when an entity is edited in ways that may affect linked stories

## Non-goals
- Real-time multi-author editing workflows
- Public reader-facing wiki pages
- Full rights/IP management system

## Acceptance criteria
- Writer can create a series and add at least two stories to it
- Writer can create shared canon entities once and link them from scenes in multiple stories
- Entity detail shows cross-story usage references with deep links
- Editing a shared entity surfaces a clear impact warning when linked scenes exist
- Existing single-story workflows remain functional for users not using series

## Priority rationale
This builds on the Campfire-inspired worldbuilding direction while creating a differentiation path for long-form series authors, a high-retention segment that needs continuity across books to stay in one tool.

## Handoff
Assign to: Market Research

title: Ship full-fidelity project backup export/import (Story Bundle)
description:
## Problem
Writers fear tool lock-in and data loss when their work includes more than manuscript text (structure, worldbuilding links, revision metadata). Current document export formats are necessary but insufficient for complete project portability and disaster recovery.

## Target user
Serious fiction writers who invest heavily in story structure and metadata and need confidence they can move or restore complete projects at any time.

## Outcome / KPI
- Increase trust and retention among users with long-running manuscripts
- Decrease support requests about migration and recovery
- Increase completion rate of backup actions per active story

## Scope
- Add `Export Story Bundle` action that packages full story data (manuscript, chapters/scenes, structure metadata, worldbuilding links, revision data)
- Add `Import Story Bundle` flow to restore a full project into the library
- Include bundle schema versioning and compatibility checks on import
- Add checksum/integrity validation with actionable error states
- Add confirmation summary before import (story name, last updated, included assets)

## Non-goals
- Replacing DOCX/PDF manuscript export workflows
- Multi-user merge conflict resolution for bundle imports
- Cross-app direct importers (e.g., Scrivener-native format) in v1

## Acceptance criteria
- Writer can export a story bundle and re-import it into a clean local library with no missing content
- Imported project preserves chapter/scene order and linked metadata relationships
- Import rejects incompatible or corrupted bundles with clear messaging
- Bundle format includes explicit version metadata for forward compatibility handling
- Export/import actions are available without requiring cloud sync opt-in

## Priority rationale
Portability and recovery trust are table stakes for writer adoption and directly support the goal to ship a credible end-to-end writing app. This feature complements manuscript export by preserving full project fidelity.

## Handoff
Assign to: Market Research

title: Story app UX polish — layout stability, visual hierarchy, tooltips
Add POV and character-presence balance dashboard for draft diagnostics
description:
## Problem
Writers often discover late that POV distribution and character presence are unintentionally imbalanced, causing pacing and perspective issues that require costly rewrite passes. Current scene metadata helps capture context but does not provide manuscript-level balance diagnostics.

## Target user
Multi-POV and ensemble-cast fiction writers who need quick visibility into perspective and presence distribution during drafting/revision.

## Outcome / KPI
- Reduce late-stage rewrites caused by accidental POV/character imbalance
- Increase usage of scene metadata during active drafting
- Increase completion rate of planned revision passes

## Scope
- Add a diagnostics dashboard summarizing POV distribution across chapters/scenes
- Add character-presence heatmap by chapter with filter by major/minor characters
- Flag configurable imbalance thresholds (e.g., POV dominance, long character absence)
- Provide deep links from each diagnostic signal to the relevant scenes
- Allow writers to dismiss or snooze specific warnings per manuscript

## Non-goals
- Auto-rewriting scenes to fix imbalance
- Prescriptive “correct” narrative formulas
- Sentiment/style scoring in this ticket

## Acceptance criteria
- Dashboard renders POV share and character-presence views for a manuscript with scene metadata
- Writer can click any flagged item and navigate to impacted scenes
- Threshold warnings are deterministic and documented in-product
- Writer can dismiss/snooze warnings and settings persist per story
- Dashboard remains usable on manuscripts with at least 100 scenes

## Priority rationale
This is a high-value revision aid that extends existing metadata work into actionable insight, helping writers produce stronger drafts faster and differentiating beyond basic structure tools.

## Handoff
Assign to: Market Research
